purpose: M16.3 launch census: per-family launch count + kernel time of the current batched B=3 verifier (the economics path), to pick the first megakernel/fusion target. 931 launches/pass, 15.97 ms kernel/pass on W7900/gfx1100.
findings: No single family dominates (>78/pass); launch count is spread ~1/layer across ~9 families. Reaching 931->400 requires fusing several.
Biggest launch bucket: paro_rotate (1+2+3) at ~146/pass -- input rotation before each W4 GEMV. Down-rotate is already fused (silu_mul_dual_rotate_out, default-on selected_moe_down_staged).
The old 120/pass runtime_memset is GONE (fillBufferAligned now 0.6/pass) -- already eliminated; do not chase.
copyBuffer D2D at 55/pass is pure plumbing (bit-exact by construction); candidate for write-through elimination.
router logits+select (77/pass = 2/layer) are a clean self-contained fusion candidate (deterministic topk -> bit-exact RED easy).
next: Cheapest-first: re-measure the existing bit-exact staged-rotate flags (HIPENGINE_SHARED_EXPERT_FUSED_ROTATE, HIPENGINE_SELECTED_MOE_STAGED_ROTATE) on the current tree (post M15.x/M16.4) for exact-AR + launches + C_B, since those changes may have shifted the kernel-time trade. Then design the router-fusion megakernel and the selected-MoE megakernel.
